text:
**IBEW Traveler Snapshot — Tuesday, July 7, 2026**

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** is running hot with 36 calls and 180 hands on the books. Meade Electric's Meta Data Center gig in Lebanon pays **$53.80/hr** (10% over scale) for 5-12-hour days Monday through Friday plus Saturdays—new construction, so the work's solid. Google Data Center in Mooresville and Eli Lilly LP1 in Lebanon are both spinning up heavy, but they're screening hard (background checks, OSHA certs, the full nine). This is where the real money is if you've got your ducks in a row.

**LU-481's book depth sits around 5:1**, meaning if you land, you're likely working.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** is a data center feeding frenzy. Four contractors—Gurtz, MJ Electric, Cupertino, and Uihlein—are all pushing Port Washington Data Center work at **JW scale plus $10/hr**, running 50–60 hour weeks. That's premium pay in the Midwest, and the volume is staggering. Drug tests required across the board, but if you pass, you're in.

**LU-237 Niagara Falls, NY** has tight inventory (7 calls, 101 hands) but Ferguson and O'Connell Electric are both hammering Lake Mariner Data at **$48.20/hr**, 7-10s, first shift. Per diem available. This isn't overpaying, but it's steady and the schedule's reasonable.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** is unique: Parsons Electric owns the Polaris Forge Data Center with three separate Inside JW calls at **$44.80/hr** plus **$100/day non-taxed per diem** (150 on weekends) if you're 100+ miles out. Second shift has 31.4% differential. Book 1 is thin (108 hands for 24 calls), so dispatch moves fast.

**LU-583 El Paso, TX** needs NM State JIW License EE98J holders badly—Rosendin, Summit Line, and Cupertino are all running projects at **$55.00/hr** with double-time OT. Specialized skills (VESDA, fire alarm) command premiums.

**The trend is unmistakable: data centers are the only game in town right now, and they're concentrated in the Midwest and Mountain West.**
